channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Father faces court after allegedly murdering wife and two sons
item.description: The 47-year-old has faced court charged with what police described as the "particularly violent" murder of his wife and sons in Sydney's south-west.

media:group.media:description: A man has been charged with murder over the death of a woman and two children in Campbelltown, in Sydney's south-west.

item.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' by AFL's call to end Tasmanian partnership
item.description: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

media:group.media:description: The AFL is forcing Hawthorn to stop playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the 2028 season.

item.title: Neymar recalled for Brazil's World Cup campaign despite three-year hiatus
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti includes Brazil's all-time leading goalscorer in his first FIFA World Cup squad.

media:group.media:description: Neymar has been included in Brazil's FIFA World Cup squad despite not playing at the international level since 2023.

item.title: Australians detained after Israel intercepts Gaza-bound flotilla
item.description: Israeli forces detain 11 Australians after its navy intercepts ships seeking to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza, flotilla organisers say.

media:group.media:description: Flotilla media delegates say Bianca Pullman-Webb is among the 11 Australians detained by Israel.

item.title: No long-term rentals but 200 short-stay listings in WA towns
item.description: There are renewed calls for a ban on short-stay rentals as a new report shows holiday homes vastly outnumber permanent rentals in small regional communities.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Loney and Gabrielle Becerra Mellet

media:group.media:description: Nannup in the South West has a high rate of short-stay accommodation.

item.title: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades' spreads to WA, Qld and SA
item.description: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".
item.dc:creator: Jason Walls

media:group.media:description: Mark Butler says the current diphtheria outbreak is Australia's biggest "certainly for decades".

item.title: Ebola treatment centres open in Congo as WHO sends team
item.description: The cash-strapped World Health Organization is sending a team of experts to the Democratic Republic of the Congo as hundreds of cases of Ebola are suspected.

media:group.media:description: Health experts say the number of cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is likely to increase.

item.title: Advocates scramble to find funding for regional tech help service
item.description: If you're having connectivity issues, one place you can turn to is the Regional Tech Hub. But now it might be forced to close.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Sullivan

media:group.media:description: The Telecommunications Industry Ombudsman received more than 50,000 complaints from regional Australia in four years.

item.title: Gippsland Nationals MP Tim Bull to exit politics at Victorian election
item.description: Tim Bull has announced he will not contest the November election, after representing Gippsland East for the National Party for 16 years.
item.dc:creator: Jarrod Whittaker

media:group.media:description: Nationals MP for Gippsland East Tim Bull will retire in November.

item.title: Detective in OJ Simpson murder trial Mark Fuhrman dies at 74
item.description: Mark Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of OJ Simpson's ex-wife, Nicole Brown Simpson, and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles.

media:group.media:description: Mark Fuhrman during OJ Simpson's murder trial in Los Angeles in the mid 90s.

item.title: Road with no name leads emergency crews astray
item.description: When Chris Stehbens spied a lost ambulance driving up and down his road during a Triple Zero (000) emergency response, he was not surprised by the struggle to find the right address.
item.dc:creator: Frazer Pearce

media:group.media:description: Chris Stehbens believes this no-name rural road could lead to a life and death emergency.

item.title: Xi to welcome Putin for visit days after hosting Trump
item.description: While this meeting comes just days after Beijing hosted the US president, the Kremlin insists there is "no correlation" between the visits.

media:group.media:description: Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping met in Beijing last September.

item.title: Driver hospitalised after 350km/h shunt in Indy 500 practice
item.description: Alexander Rossi has been taken to hospital after a heavy crash during Indy 500 practice that saw two other cars wrecked just days out from the most prestigious race on the American calendar.

media:group.media:description: Alexander Rossi was hospitalised after this crash.

item.title: Arsenal edges closer to Premier League title despite more VAR drama
item.description: Kai Havertz emerges Arsenal's hero after scoring the winner against Burnley, and is spared a sending-off for a horror tackle.

media:group.media:description: Kai Havertz scored Arsenal's winner against Burley, but was lucky to avoid a sending off later on in the match.

item.title: Strong catch numbers end banana prawn season early for major operator
item.description: The largest operator in Australia's most valuable prawn fishery wraps up banana prawn season a month early because catch numbers were so high.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson

media:group.media:description: Peter Adams says his seafood store in Cairns is well-stocked with banana prawns.

item.title: Spotting these AI relationship warning signs may avoid a delusional spiral
item.description: Intensive chatbot use may be amplifying dangerous and harmful delusions for some users. Here are some of the "red flags" for "AI psychosis".
item.dc:creator: James Purtill

media:group.media:description: Research shows chatbots sustain delusional thinking initiated by a user, creating a feedback loop.

item.title: Court shutdown means justice takes much longer in Balgo
item.description: In this remote East Kimberley community, where the local court has been shut for almost two years due to security concerns, the closure of the nearest regional court from June 1 is raising concerns about justice.
item.dc:creator: Dunja Karagic

media:group.media:description: Balgo is a remote Aboriginal community in the Western Desert.

item.title: Historic flour mill walls reduced to rubble after failed heritage bid
item.description: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.
item.dc:creator: Samuel O'Brien and Elyse Armanini

media:group.media:description: The historic flour mill walls crumble amid demolition works.

item.title: Boomers star Joe Ingles thrilled to come 'home' in coup NBL signing
item.description: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Joe Ingles has just finished a two-year stint with the NBA's Minnesota Timberwolves.

item.title: Married at First Sight UK off air after rape allegations
item.description: British broadcaster Channel 4 removes all previous seasons of Married at First Sight after rape allegations come to light.

media:group.media:description: All 10 seasons of Married at First Sight UK have been removed from Channel 4's streaming platform.

item.title: Tomic edges closer to French Open amid Aussie qualifying nightmare
item.description: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching his first main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

media:group.media:description: Bernard Tomic was the only one of five Australian players to have a successful day in the French Open qualifiers.

item.title: Community fights back against caravan park development proposal
item.description: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope, Lily Kristanto, and Rachael Lucas

media:group.media:description: Daniel Gunton says it is disappointing caravanners and campers will not be able to stay in the town.

item.title: Live: ASX jumps more than 1 per cent in rebound rally
item.description: The local share market is rebounding from Monday's heavy selling, as shares in Taus make back some of their heavy losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ziffer

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: 'Morale is low': Grocery, butcher's set alight in Melbourne shopping strip
item.description: Fire crews were called to a blaze in Glenroy that gutted several shops. Locals say there have been multiple suspicious fires in the area.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Ferrier

media:group.media:description: CCTV from inside one of the shops shows several people wearing face masks entering the site prior to the fire at Glenroy butcher and grocery.

item.title: Live: More jet fuel secured from China, Albanese says
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces the federal government has secured 600,000 barrels of jet fuel from China. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese speaking at a Perth press conference.
